title: Three Questions
summary: Kashkari frames his policy view around three questions: how to reconcile a weakening labor market with exuberant financial markets, whether inflation expectations are at risk of unanchoring, and which tail risk is more likely. He leans toward the labor-market downside being the bigger risk and therefore supported cutting the funds rate at the September meeting, while stressing the cut is not a preset path. He reads inflation as stuck near 3 percent and likely tariff-driven but more a persistence risk than a jump to 4-5 percent, and he judges expectations still anchored. A recurring theme is a higher neutral rate (R*), partly from the AI/data-center investment boom and pricier foreign capital, which implies policy has been less restrictive than thought and that rate cuts may not lower long rates much. He explicitly keeps the door open to pausing or even hiking if the labor market proves resilient or inflation surprises up.

key_quotes.quote: Putting these questions and their potential answers together led me to support cutting the federal funds rate at this week’s FOMC meeting.
key_quotes.context: His near-term decision to back a cut, driven by labor-market risk

key_quotes.quote: For me the more likely risk is a rapid further weakening of the labor market.
key_quotes.context: Question 3 — judges the employment side as the binding concern

key_quotes.quote: I do not believe we should be on a preset course for a series of rate cuts. If the labor market proves more resilient than it seems at the moment or if inflation surprises to the upside, we should be prepared to pause and hold our policy rate. I even remain open to raising the policy rate further if economic conditions warrant it.
key_quotes.context: Caveats the dovish cut with explicit two-sided optionality

key_quotes.quote: The implication of this reassessment is that monetary policy has likely not been as tight as I previously understood.
key_quotes.context: Higher-R* read that lowers his estimate of how restrictive current policy is
